title: urticaceae
intro: The urticaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the nettle family. This family is characterized by its diverse range of species, including stinging nettles, dock leaves, and jewelweed. The urticaceae family comprises approximately 264 genera and 3,000 species, many of which are found in temperate and subtropical regions worldwide. These plants are often known for their unique adaptations, such as the stinging hairs found on the stinging nettle, which serve as a defense mechanism against herbivores and other predators.
